meta.title: ERC EDW ingress — seven-approach figures with license vs infrastructure split
meta.purpose: Machine-readable source of truth for the full 7-approach comparison. License, infrastructure, and labor are distinct streams, each with capex (one-time) and monthly (recurring). 3-yr TCO = capex + 36 x monthly.
meta.cost_basis: Cloud: live Azure Retail (West US, 2026-07-22) + published OCI list. License list prices 2026 (Oracle DB EE $47,500/proc, SE2 ~$17,500/socket, +22%/yr support; SQL 2022 Std $3,944 / Ent $15,122 per 2-core pack, +~25%/yr SA; OIC ~$943/mo/pack; ADW OCPU $4.0288 Lic-Incl / $1.3441 BYOL; Oracle GoldenGate perpetual $17,500/proc, OCI GoldenGate managed $0.6721/OCPU-hr Lic-Incl / $0.1613 BYOL). On-prem hardware/labor are documented planning estimates.
meta.caveat: Planning estimates; not a quote. Enterprise license discounts (50-80% off list) and cloud reservations (30-41% off) are NOT applied. Blended labor $150/hr. Model: seven_approach_cost_model.py.
meta.scope_invariant: Every approach lands its output in the Oracle EDW STAGING zone. Power BI consumption is DOWNSTREAM and INVARIANT: Agiline's aiWorks loads the Oracle EDW from staging (running Billow's PL/SQL), then Billow consumes the Oracle EDW into Power BI. Power BI never reads the ingestion substrate, so BI-platform 'nativeness' (Fabric/OneLake/Power BI) is NOT an ingress-tier differentiator — do not use it to select or rank an approach.
meta.presentation_rule: The three MANAGED options (ADF/Fabric/Databricks) sit within a labor-estimate NOISE BAND of ~$159k-$231k; present them as a BAND with ADF at the low edge, NOT as an ordinal ranking. Only the CROSS-FAMILY gaps (managed band vs OCI-native vs roll-your-own vs on-prem) are large enough to survive the labor assumptions and may be stated as ordered.

3_fabric.label: Fabric (Azure managed)
3_fabric.note: F16 PAYG basis. F8-reserved best-case ~$101k run+build (v0.2) is CONDITIONAL on fitting an F8 SKU. Judge on cost + Oracle-sink maturity + capacity ONLY; Power BI nativeness is not a differentiator (scope_invariant).

5_oci_native.label: Oracle OCI-native (Lic-Incl)
5_oci_native.like_for_like_comparator: 5b_oci_byol ($232,716) is the correct comparator to Azure, not this Lic-Incl figure

5b_oci_byol.label: OCI-native (BYOL)
5b_oci_byol.note: ADW at BYOL rate; license = OIC only; assumes owned Oracle DB licenses. THE like-for-like comparator to Azure — ~$30k above ADF, level with Fabric, below Databricks/roll-your-own.

6b_onprem_oracle_ee.label: On-prem Oracle EE
6b_onprem_oracle_ee.note: EE license alone (~$315k/3yr) exceeds the entire ADF TCO

contingencies.note: Conditional lines NOT in the base TCO above. Toggle per PoC finding. Both are Azure-family exposures that OCI-native avoids entirely.

goldengate_if_telematics_cdc.applies_when: ONLY if the telematics lane needs low-latency CDC. The DEFAULT batch landing (P2: Parquet -> OCI Object Storage + DBMS_CLOUD) needs NO GoldenGate and is most of the workload.
goldengate_if_telematics_cdc.poc_exit_question: Does telematics require GoldenGate/CDC, or does micro-batch P2 suffice?

cross_cloud_egress_azure_to_oci.materiality: immaterial to TCO in dollars; the one line OCI-native zeroes -> qualitative point in OCI-native's favor, not a budget-mover

ops_decomposition.note: Ops-labor splits into a SHARED connector-drift slice (roughly constant across approaches) and an ENGINE-upkeep slice (the part that differs). ADF's advantage is 'near-zero ENGINE upkeep', not 'lowest TOTAL ops'.
ops_decomposition.assumption: 30 sources x 2 breaking changes/source/yr x 2h/change (editable lever CONNECTOR_CHANGES_PER_SOURCE_YR)
ops_decomposition.readout: At 2 changes/source/yr the shared slice (~10h/mo) already approaches/exceeds ADF's assumed 8h/mo TOTAL — so if connector volatility is high, all four Azure approaches' realized ops converge upward.

ranking_note: See meta.presentation_rule. The array below is TCO-sorted for reference only; the three managed entries (1_adf, 3_fabric, 2_databricks) are a BAND, not an order.
key_findings: SCOPE INVARIANT: every approach lands in Oracle EDW staging; Power BI consumes the Oracle EDW downstream of Agiline's aiWorks load, invariant across approaches — so BI-platform (Fabric/Power BI) nativeness is NOT an ingress-tier differentiator.
The three managed options are a BAND (~$159k-$231k), inside the labor-estimate noise; ADF sits at the low edge. Present as a band, not a ranking. Only cross-family gaps are decisive.

License is an explicit separable SKU on-prem and in Oracle OCI; hyperscalers bundle it into the service rate. Fabric is ~41% license (capacity subscription); Databricks DBU is license on rented infra; roll-your-own is only 2% license (traded for 90% labor).
On-prem TCO (~$506k) is dominated by infrastructure (hardware ~$210k/3yr) + labor (~$162k/3yr), not license — for Standard/SE2 tiers.
Oracle Enterprise Edition license alone (~$315k/3yr) exceeds the entire Azure-managed ADF TCO (~$159k).

OCI-native: compare at BYOL (~$233k), the like-for-like case (owned Oracle DB licenses). It is ~$30k above ADF and level with Fabric. Prefer Azure over it on WORKLOAD ISOLATION (don't run ETL on the ADW that serves reporting) + connector breadth — not on TCO, where the gap is not decisive.

GoldenGate is uncosted in the base and may be REQUIRED for telematics CDC (~$8k-58k/3yr depending on managed BYOL vs perpetual; ~$35k central). It applies to every Azure approach and is zero for OCI-native — a specific point in OCI-native's favor. PoC exit question.
Cross-cloud egress is immaterial in dollars (~$365/3yr) but is the one line OCI-native zeroes — qualitative, not a budget-mover.
Cross-family ranking (survives the assumptions): managed band ($159k-$231k) < OCI-native BYOL $233k / Lic-Incl $256k < Roll-your-own $388k < On-prem SE2/SQL ~$506k < Oracle EE $763k.
